WMS-Rot Suite

WMS-Wavefunctions

Inspect rotational eigenstates, level structure, and directional wavefunction maps exported by WMS-Rot.

No data loaded.

Equations Used For Rotational Treatment

$$\psi_{nJM}(\alpha,\beta,\gamma)=\sum_{K=-J}^{J} c_{JKM}^{(n)}\,D^{J*}_{MK}(\alpha,\beta,\gamma)$$

$$\psi_{J\alpha}=\sum_{K=-J}^{J}c_{JK}^{(\alpha)}\,|J,K\rangle,\qquad \alpha\ \text{labels asymmetric-top eigenstates at fixed }J$$

$$\Psi_{J\alpha}^{(M)}(\theta,\phi)=\sum_{K=-J}^{J} c_{JK}^{(\alpha)}\,d^J_{MK}(\theta)e^{iK\phi}$$

Wavefunction meanings (rigorous, in this viewer):

  • \( \psi_{nJM}(\alpha,\beta,\gamma) \): full rigid-rotor eigenfunction in Euler angles \((\alpha,\beta,\gamma)\), expanded on Wigner-\(D\) functions.
  • \( \psi_{J\alpha}=\sum_K c_{JK}^{(\alpha)}|J,K\rangle \): asymmetric-top state at fixed \(J\), written in the symmetric-top basis \(|J,K\rangle\); \(\alpha\) is the internal label after diagonalization.
  • \( \Psi_{J\alpha}^{(M)}(\theta,\phi) \): fixed-\(M\) directional amplitude used by this map; \((\theta,\phi)\) are polar angles of \(Z_{\text{lab}}\) in the molecule-fixed \(A/B/C\) frame.

$$P_M(\theta,\phi)=\left|\Psi_{J\alpha}^{(M)}(\theta,\phi)\right|^2,\qquad r(\theta,\phi)=\left[\frac{P_M(\theta,\phi)}{P_{M,\max}}\right]^\gamma$$

$$\hat{\mathbf{u}}=(\sin\theta\cos\phi,\sin\theta\sin\phi,\cos\theta),\qquad \mathbf{Q}^{(\mathrm{lab})}=\frac{\int P_M(\theta,\phi)\,\hat{\mathbf{u}}\hat{\mathbf{u}}^{\mathsf T}\,d\Omega}{\int P_M(\theta,\phi)\,d\Omega},\qquad [\hat{\mathbf{n}}_{X_\mathrm{lab}},\hat{\mathbf{n}}_{Y_\mathrm{lab}},\hat{\mathbf{n}}_{Z_\mathrm{lab}}]=\mathrm{eigvecs}\!\left(\mathbf{Q}^{(\mathrm{lab})}\right),\ \lambda_Z\ge\lambda_X\ge\lambda_Y$$

$$\mathbf{v}_{\mathrm{lab}}=\mathbf{R}_{\mathrm{lab}\leftarrow\mathrm{body}}\,\mathbf{v}_{\mathrm{body}},\qquad \mathbf{R}_{\mathrm{lab}\leftarrow\mathrm{body}}= \begin{bmatrix} \hat{\mathbf{n}}_{X_\mathrm{lab}}^{\mathsf T}\\ \hat{\mathbf{n}}_{Y_\mathrm{lab}}^{\mathsf T}\\ \hat{\mathbf{n}}_{Z_\mathrm{lab}}^{\mathsf T} \end{bmatrix}, \quad \mathbf{R}^{-1}=\mathbf{R}^{\mathsf T}$$

$$\mathbf{u}_J:\ u_{J,i}=\sqrt{\frac{\langle J_i^2\rangle}{J(J+1)}}\ (i=A,B,C),\qquad \hat{\mathbf{n}}_F^{\mathrm{eff}}=\mathrm{sgn}(c_J)\,\frac{\mathbf{u}_J}{\lVert\mathbf{u}_J\rVert}$$

$$c_J=\frac{\langle\mathbf{J}\!\cdot\!\mathbf{F}\rangle}{F(F+1)}=\frac{F(F+1)+J(J+1)-I(I+1)}{2F(F+1)},\qquad c_{I_\mathrm{eff}}=\frac{\langle\mathbf{I}_{\mathrm{eff}}\!\cdot\!\mathbf{F}\rangle}{F(F+1)}=\frac{F(F+1)+I_{\mathrm{eff}}(I_{\mathrm{eff}}+1)-J(J+1)}{2F(F+1)}$$

$$I_\mathrm{eff}=I\ \text{(one quadrupolar nucleus)},\qquad I_\mathrm{eff}=I_{12}\ \text{(two quadrupolar nuclei, dominant coupled-spin label of the selected }F\text{ level)}$$

$$\mathbf{I}_{12}=\mathbf{I}_1+\mathbf{I}_2,\qquad F=J+I_\mathrm{eff},\qquad \cos\theta_{JF}=\frac{F(F+1)+J(J+1)-I_\mathrm{eff}(I_\mathrm{eff}+1)}{2\sqrt{F(F+1)J(J+1)}}$$

$$\cos\theta_{I_\mathrm{eff}F}=\frac{F(F+1)+I_\mathrm{eff}(I_\mathrm{eff}+1)-J(J+1)}{2\sqrt{F(F+1)I_\mathrm{eff}(I_\mathrm{eff}+1)}}$$

$$\begin{aligned} \hat{\mathbf{n}}_J(\varphi)&=\cos\theta_{JF}\,\hat{\mathbf{n}}_F^{\mathrm{eff}}+\sin\theta_{JF}\,(\cos\varphi\,\hat{\mathbf{e}}_1+\sin\varphi\,\hat{\mathbf{e}}_2),\\ \hat{\mathbf{n}}_{I_\mathrm{eff}}(\varphi)&=\cos\theta_{I_\mathrm{eff}F}\,\hat{\mathbf{n}}_F^{\mathrm{eff}}-\sin\theta_{I_\mathrm{eff}F}\,(\cos\varphi\,\hat{\mathbf{e}}_1+\sin\varphi\,\hat{\mathbf{e}}_2),\quad \{\hat{\mathbf{e}}_1,\hat{\mathbf{e}}_2\}\perp\hat{\mathbf{n}}_F^{\mathrm{eff}} \end{aligned}$$

Viewer quantity: direction-probability map of \(Z_{\text{lab}}\) expressed in the molecule-fixed \(A/B/C\) frame, using the selected \(M\) component (default \(M=0\)).

If hyperfine levels are present, labels include \(F\). The 3D shape is drawn from the dominant parent rotational component \((J,\alpha)\), with reported purity.

Quantum-mechanical caveat (rigorous): the plotted \(J/I/F\) lines and cones are expectation-value/semi-classical constructions. They summarize operator-coupling geometry and become directly intuitive in the semiclassical (large-angular-momentum) limit; they are not simultaneous sharp vector observables of one exact stationary eigenstate.

How To Read The Viewer

  1. Left panel: each horizontal segment is one energy level. If hyperfine is active, labels include \(F\).
  2. Right panel: this is an angular map, not atomic/electronic density in Cartesian space.
  3. It shows where \(Z_{\text{lab}}\) points in the molecule-fixed \(A/B/C\) frame, for the selected \(M\) component.
  4. The frame toggle changes only plotting coordinates: Body frame draws directly in \(A/B/C\); Lab frame applies the orthonormal rotation \(R_{\text{lab}\leftarrow\text{body}}\).
  5. Surface radius and color follow normalized \( |\Psi|^2 \). Teal line = active \(K\)-axis. Orange line = \(\mathbf{u}_J\) from \(\langle J_i^2\rangle\). Black/gray lines = principal \(X_{\text{lab}},Y_{\text{lab}},Z_{\text{lab}}\) from eigvecs\((\mathbf{Q}^{(\mathrm{lab})})\).
  6. If \(F\) is available, dark-blue line is the effective \(F\)-axis and translucent cones are the semiclassical \(J\)- and \(I_\mathrm{eff}\)-precession cones; with two nuclei \(I_\mathrm{eff}=I_{12}\).
  7. A longer lobe along A (or B/C) means \(Z_{\text{lab}}\) is more probable near \(\pm\)A (or \(\pm\)B/\(\pm\)C).
  8. Changing \(M\) changes nodal pattern and anisotropy because \(M\) is the projection of \(J\) on \(Z_{\text{lab}}\).

Energy Levels

State F I / I12 Energy (cm^-1)

Wavefunction Map (Selected M, Z_lab direction)

M = projection of J on Z_lab
0.75
Frame:

Body frame: colored axes are molecule-fixed A/B/C and black/gray lines are lab principal axes from \(Q^{(\mathrm{lab})}\). Lab frame: colored axes are \(X_\mathrm{lab}/Y_\mathrm{lab}/Z_\mathrm{lab}\) and black/gray lines are the rotated body A/B/C axes. Teal line = active K-axis. Orange line = \(\mathbf{u}_J\). Dark-blue line = effective \(F\)-axis; cones are semiclassical \(J/I_\mathrm{eff}\) precession around \(F\), with \(I_\mathrm{eff}=I_{12}\) when two nuclei are active.
